ordering_note: CRITICAL: -A PREPENDS (inserts at front), -a APPENDS (f3 section 2.3, auditctl(8) -A/-a). The textual line 2 (-A ...never) is INSERTED in FRONT of line 1 (-a ...always). Effective match order: [never (uid=0), always]. So the never rule wins for uid=0 and SUPPRESSES root execve, exactly like scenario 9 but achieved via -A prepend rather than file position. A cost model that orders rules by TEXT LINE rather than by -A/-a insertion semantics gets the suppression direction WRONG.

rules.note: PREPENDED (-A) so it sits at the FRONT of the list, before the always rule. Action never => suppressive. Suppresses uid=0 execve. Contributes 0 additive volume.
APPENDED; sits behind the prepended never rule. Net is reduced by the suppressed uid=0 slice (workload-dependent).

dominant_tier: HIGH (additive), with a prepended NEGATIVE/suppressive rule reducing it
rate_band_basis: f3 section 6: always execve HIGH; the prepended never is 0 (suppressive). Contrast scenario 33 (never AFTER always does NOT suppress).
